title: Image Metadata Reader
shortDescription: Read EXIF photo metadata online, including camera details, resolution, timestamps and GPS when available.
metaTitle: Image Metadata Reader Online | Read EXIF Data Fast
metaDescription: Read EXIF and image metadata online. Check camera model, date taken, resolution, GPS location, file size and other photo details instantly.
intro: Read photo metadata online in your browser. This EXIF reader shows camera details, capture time, resolution, GPS coordinates and other embedded image information without installing software or uploading the file to a server.
overview: Use this online image metadata reader to inspect EXIF data from photos quickly and privately. Upload an image and view camera model, lens, exposure settings, image size, date taken and location data when available. It is especially useful for photographers, editors, marketplace uploads and anyone checking image details before sharing, publishing or archiving.
howItWorks: Upload a photo from your device.
The tool scans embedded EXIF and metadata in the browser.
Review camera, date, resolution, orientation and GPS fields.
Copy the data or use it to verify image provenance.
useCases: Check camera settings from a photo.
Find when and where an image was taken.
Inspect metadata before removing it for privacy.
Troubleshoot file details for editing or archiving.

examples.title: Camera info check
examples.input: Upload a JPEG from your phone
examples.output: Camera model, exposure, aperture, ISO and focal length are displayed.
examples.description: Helpful for reviewing shooting settings before editing, publishing or archiving.

examples.title: Location lookup
examples.input: Open a photo with GPS tags
examples.output: Latitude and longitude appear if embedded in the file.
examples.description: Useful for travel, event and field photos when location data matters.

faq.question: What metadata can this tool read?
faq.answer: It can read common EXIF fields such as camera model, date taken, resolution, orientation, exposure settings, file size and GPS data if present.

faq.question: Does it work in the browser?
faq.answer: Yes. The reader processes images client-side in your browser for fast and private metadata inspection.

faq.question: Why do some photos show no metadata?
faq.answer: Some images have metadata removed by the camera, app or export settings, or the file format may not contain EXIF data.

faq.question: Can I find GPS location in every image?
faq.answer: No. GPS data only appears if it was saved in the original file and not stripped later.
